mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or held at the zero lower bound, equity discount rates stay compressed and risk appetite improves, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal fails if rate cuts coincide with severe growth deterioration that overwhelms the valuation channel.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered months do not show higher 20d S&P 500 returns than non-triggered months, or if the rolling 3-year hit rate falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE of at least 0.3 percentage points over three months signals tightening labor conditions and weaker household income growth, which should push S&P 500 returns negative over the next month. The signal fails when unemployment rises from a low base during a strong productivity-led expansion.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward returns are not negative in a majority of UNRATE spike windows, or if the sign of the effect reverses in a sustained expansion regime.

mechanism_narrative: A joint rise in RSAFS and PAYEMS indicates broadening nominal demand and hiring, which supports revenue growth expectations and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism fails if sales rise but employment contracts, implying inventory or pricing effects rather than real demand strength.
failure_condition: Reject if instances with concurrent 3-month gains in RSAFS and PAYEMS do not outperform other periods on 60d S&P 500 returns, or if interaction terms add no predictive power out of sample.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO turns up while HOUST remains weak, it signals industrial recovery without a broad housing-led credit impulse, which should generate modest positive S&P 500 returns over the next week through better cyclical sentiment. The signal fails if industrial gains are purely inventory restocking and do not translate into earnings revisions.
failure_condition: Reject if short-horizon returns are not positive when INDPRO is rising and HOUST is flat or falling, or if the combined signal is subsumed by either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL acceleration above 0.4 percent month over month while FEDFUNDS remains below 1 percent implies negative real rates and persistent policy accommodation, which supports positive equity returns over the next month. The mechanism fails if inflation surges because of demand destruction or policy credibility loss.
failure_condition: Reject if positive inflation surprises under low FEDFUNDS do not correspond to above-baseline 20d S&P 500 returns, or if returns turn negative in most such episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in GDPC1 confirms output contraction and weaker final demand, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as earnings expectations are revised down. The signal fails when GDP weakness is already fully priced before the observation date.
failure_condition: Reject if quarters with negative GDPC1 growth are not followed by below-median 60d S&P 500 returns, or if the effect disappears after excluding recession periods.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous deterioration in PAYEMS and rise in UNRATE signals a genuine labor-market break, which should drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next month through weaker consumption and earnings expectations. The signal fails if labor data weaken only because of temporary payroll volatility while unemployment stays stable.
failure_condition: Reject if months with falling PAYEMS and rising UNRATE do not show negative 20d returns more often than not, or if either proxy alone explains the effect equally well.

mechanism_narrative: In a housing-led recovery, rising HOUST signals stronger construction activity, credit creation, and durable-goods demand, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism fails if housing starts rise only because of temporary supply normalization without broad demand improvement.
failure_condition: Reject if above-trend HOUST months are not followed by higher 60d S&P 500 returns than baseline, or if the signal reverses during housing slowdowns.
